methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Pano AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Sage Geosystems

private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $120M

data.narrative:
Nyobolt, a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by Sai Shivareddy and Clare Grey, is evaluated against a comparison universe of five private peers in the same sector and stage, supplemented by one public comp (Lanzatech, LNZA). The company has raised $100M+ to date.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $100M total raised as a proxy for current value, applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $190M. Peer-median methodology examines five same-sector, same-stage companies—Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and Cemvita ($120M)—producing a median valuation of $310M with a range of $120M to $500M. The median and minimum valuations among peers reflect material dispersion, suggesting heterogeneous investor views on risk and technology readiness within the cohort.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $162M (low) to $357M (high), with a median implied value of $250M. This midpoint reflects the tension between stage-based progression and peer comparables; Nyobolt's raised capital suggests earlier positioning relative to the peer median, while the peer set's broad range ($120M–$500M) underscores uncertainty in commercialization risk and technology differentiation. Key uncertainties center on capital intensity trajectories and regulatory pathway clarity within the climate tech sector.
